title: PM Modi honoured at Seychelles national day; India expands bilateral ties with UPI, ₹1,250 crore line of credit and more
content:
• India extended an umbrella Line of Credit agreement worth ₹1,250 crore (in rupees) to Seychelles to support priority development projects.
• The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) and the Central Bank of Seychelles signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to facilitate the introduction of India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) in Seychelles.
• India and Seychelles signed an extradition treaty aimed at combating transnational crime.

title: Venezuela earthquakes: Satellite images show significant destruction after twin quakes; death toll nears 1500
content:
• Venezuela was struck by two powerful earthquakes with magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5, causing widespread devastation.
• The earthquakes resulted in a death toll of 1,450, with 3,150 people injured, and displaced 12,721 residents in La Guaira.
• At least 774 buildings collapsed in La Guaira, Venezuela, prompting 24 nations to send international aid and rescue personnel.

title: What is birthright citizenship? Understanding Trump's executive order and the US Supreme Court case
content:
• President Donald Trump signed Executive Order 14160, titled "Protecting the Meaning and Value of American Citizenship," on January 20, 2025, aiming to restrict birthright citizenship.
• The US Supreme Court heard oral arguments on April 1, 2026, in the case *Trump v. Barbara*, which challenges the executive order's consistency with the Citizenship Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.
• The established interpretation of birthright citizenship largely stems from the Supreme Court's landmark 1898 decision in *United States v. Wong Kim Ark*, which held that nearly everyone born on US soil automatically becomes a US citizen.

title: US Supreme Court to decide birthright citizenship, election and transgender athletes cases: What to know
content:
• The US Supreme Court is reviewing cases concerning the President's authority to remove officials from independent federal agencies like the Federal Reserve and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), potentially overturning a 91-year-old precedent.
• Specific disputes before the Supreme Court regarding presidential power to dismiss independent federal agency leaders involve Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook and former FTC Commissioner Rebecca Slaughter.
• The Supreme Court is considering a case that could eliminate post-election grace periods for mail ballots in federal elections, currently allowed in 29 states, potentially affecting millions of voters, if they rule that ballots must arrive by Election Day.

title: Plan afoot to create index for unincorporated enterprises
content:
• The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) plans to launch a new index for unincorporated sector enterprises, with an official timeline of 2031, to provide insights into the informal sector.
• According to the Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises (ASUSE), the number of unincorporated establishments increased by 8% to 79.2 million in 2025 from 73.4 million in 2023-24, with employment rising to 128.1 million.
• The Ministry is also set to launch the Index of Service Production next month, a new high-frequency indicator for the services sector, with FY25 as the base year.

title: As GST turns 10, focus shifts to AI-led compliance, faster refunds, simpler tax processes
content:
• GST was introduced on July 1, 2017, replacing a complex system of 17 central and state taxes and 13 cesses with a unified indirect tax framework.
• The Goods and Services Tax was launched by then President Pranab Mukherjee on July 1, 2017, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi terming it a 'good and simple tax', and then Finance Minister Arun Jaitley playing a key role in its negotiations.
• Starting September 22, 2025, a next-gen GST with a two-tier structure was launched, featuring 5 per cent for essential items, 18 per cent for standard goods and services, and a separate 40 per cent slab for luxury and demerit goods.

title: As GST turns 10, focus shifts to AI-led compliance, faster refunds, simpler tax processes
content:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was introduced on July 1, 2017, by then President Pranab Mukherjee, replacing a complex system of 17 central and state taxes and 13 cesses.
• The average monthly GST collection increased from Rs 89,700 crore in 2017-18 to Rs 1.85 lakh crore in FY26, with the gross GST revenue for the 2025-26 fiscal year reaching Rs 22.27 lakh crore.
• Five petroleum products—crude petroleum, petrol, diesel, Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF), and natural gas—are currently outside the GST framework, and their inclusion is left to the GST Council to decide.

title: SIT formed to probe Dudhwa tigress’ ‘sudden’ death; NTCA seeks report
content:
• A special investigation team (SIT) was formed on Monday, June 29, 2026, to probe the death of a tigress in the Dudhwa Tiger Reserve buffer zone, headed by Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) Lalit Kumar Verma, with Additional PCCF A.P. Singh and veterinarian Utkarsh Shukla as members.
• The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), the apex body overseeing Project Tiger, whose Member Secretary is Sanjay Kumar Pathak, has sought a detailed report on the tigress' death, including compliance with NTCA's Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
• Preliminary findings from the Indian Veterinary Research Institute (IVRI) in Bareilly, after a post-mortem, identified "haemorrhagic gastritis and severe parasitic infestation" as the probable cause of the tigress' sudden death.

title: Forest department takes over 42.72 hectares of land from private estate in Kolukkumalai
content:
• The Forest department has reclaimed 42.72 hectares of ecologically sensitive land from Harrisons Malayalam Limited (HML) near Kolukkumalai, Idukki.
• The land reclamation was carried out under the Kerala Private Forests (Vesting and Assignment) Act.
• The 42.72-hectare tract was retained by the Forest department following a 1987 High Court verdict, which arose from an initial takeover of 210.89 hectares from HML in 1975.

title: MoU signed for indigenous chips in CCTV cameras
content:
• Mindgrove Technologies (IIT Madras Research Park) and Prama India Private Limited have signed an MoU to deploy indigenously designed chips in CCTV surveillance cameras.
• Mindgrove's proprietary Vision SoC, the indigenously designed chip, is funded under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology’s (Meity) Design Linked Incentive scheme.
• The collaboration aims to reduce India's reliance on imported chipsets in sensitive domains such as security and surveillance, aligning with the Government of India’s Atmanirbhar Bharat vision.
